arguments.query: prometheus client_golang promhttp handler metrics endpoint Go

turns.content:
1. Prometheus Go client library - https://github.com/prometheus/client_golang
   Official Prometheus instrumentation library for Go.
2. Instrumenting a Go application - https://prometheus.io/docs/guides/go-application/
   Step-by-step guide to adding Prometheus metrics to Go apps.
3. promhttp package - https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus/promhttp
   HTTP handler for Prometheus metrics endpoint.

arguments.title: Prometheus Go client instrumentation
arguments.content:
go get github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus/promhttp
Endpoint: http.Handle("/metrics", promhttp.Handler())
Counter: prometheus.NewCounterVec(CounterOpts{Name, Help}, []string{labels})
Histogram: prometheus.NewHistogramVec(HistogramOpts{Name, Help, Buckets}, labels)
Register: prometheus.MustRegister(metric)
Inc: counter.WithLabelValues(vals...).Inc()
Observe: histogram.WithLabelValues(vals...).Observe(duration.Seconds())

turns.content: Finding recorded: Prometheus Go client instrumentation

arguments.summary: Researched Prometheus Go client. Will add /metrics endpoint with promhttp.Handler and custom request metrics.

next_steps.description: Create internal/metrics/metrics.go with request counter and duration histogram
Add promhttp.Handler() to /metrics route in main.go
Run go mod tidy to fetch dependency

arguments.content:
package main

import (
	"log"
	"net/http"
	"metrics-collector/internal/api"
	_ "metrics-collector/internal/metrics"
	"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus/promhttp"
)

func main() {
	mux := http.NewServeMux()
	api.RegisterRoutes(mux)
	mux.Handle("/metrics", promhttp.Handler())
	log.Println("Starting server on :8080")
	log.Fatal(http.ListenAndServe(":8080", mux))
}

arguments.command: cd /project && go mod tidy 2>&1 && go build ./... 2>&1

arguments.message: Prometheus metrics endpoint is live at GET /metrics. I've defined two custom metrics: http_requests_total (counter) and http_request_duration_seconds (histogram). The metrics package is imported with a blank identifier so init() registers them. You'll want to instrument your API handlers to call RequestsTotal.Inc() and RequestDuration.Observe() — want me to add that middleware?

arguments.summary: Added Prometheus /metrics endpoint with promhttp.Handler. Created custom request counter and duration histogram metrics. Go builds cleanly with dependency.
arguments.final_answer: Prometheus metrics endpoint added at /metrics. Custom metrics defined: http_requests_total (counter) and http_request_duration_seconds (histogram). Import the metrics package in your handlers to record values.
